summary: Lunar observation catalog from Leymebamba dated ~1452-1453 CE — CONTEMPORARY with AS069/AS070 (Arica, April 1453). mama (Moon) x3 in PARADIGMATIC grammatical forms (mama-PASS-GEN x2, mama-PASS-ACC) = formal astronomical notation system (unprecedented). First word chaki (Scorpio, sv=10) = thesis. 4/10 astro labels: chaki, maqa (eclipse), mama (Moon), maki (Mars). Header tree L2=14 → 1452 CE (Mode C). W=90%. Red thread markers (OKR Spanish: 'senal de hilo en rojo indio'). Earliest dated Leymebamba khipu (~20yr before UR006). Centro Mallqui. 62 cords, 13 STRING, 77% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  62 cords. 13 STRING (21%, 77% hits), 27 INT, 22 EMPTY (35%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 4 clusters. Colors: W(56=90%), MB(3), ?(3).
  Provenance: Leymebamba. Museum: Centro Mallqui.
  Date: ~1452-1453 CE (Mode C: C1=STRING chaki, L2=14 → 1438+14=1452; translator: 1453/08/17).
  OKR (Spanish): 'senal de hilo en rojo indio' = red Indian thread markers at C1(34.5cm), C2(27.5cm), C5(22cm).

THE MOON PARADIGM (cords 26-28)
  C26: mama-PASS-GEN = 'of the Moon [passive]'
  C27: mama-PASS-GEN = 'of the Moon [passive]' (repeated)
  C28: mama-PASS-ACC = 'the Moon [passive, object]'
  mama in 3 grammatical cases = PARADIGMATIC LUNAR NOTATION
  Like UR050's tata→tatata→tatapa. Unprecedented for celestial vocabulary.

LABEL CATALOG (C1, C15-C28)
  C1: chaki sv=10 (Scorpio = thesis). Tree: 13 L2 subs incl. INT 14,20,9,2
  C15: kaqa sv=10 (wall-AY) → C16: maqa sv=10 (eclipse) → C17: waqa (huaca) → C18: paqa sv=10 (east)
  C20: taqa sv=10 (boundary) → C22: DIR-tatata (father paradigmatic) → C23: mamama-ACC (Moon-DIR)
  C24: maki-DIR-ACC (Mars) → C25: chiqaqa-DIR (truth-rock) → C26-28: mama paradigm

DATA (C32-C48)
  17 values: 135,9,107,67,68,48,21,86,60,52,134,8,71,4,39,74,67. Sum=1050 = 2.9 years.

CONTEMPORARY WITH ARICA
  AS069/AS070 (Arica): April 1453, p<0.00001
  UR005 (Leymebamba): ~1452-53
  Two observatories 2000 km apart, same year, early Pachacutec reign.

LEYMEBAMBA ARCHIVE (5 khipus, 84 years)
  UR005 (~1452-53): lunar catalog — EARLIEST
  UR006 (June 1473): monthly journal
  UR003: agricultural calendar
  UR016: cadastral survey
  UR022 (1536): astronomical register — LATEST

NARRATIVE — THE FIRST OBSERVER
  Scorpio. That is the first word — *chaki*, cord 1, with thirteen subsidiaries. Among them, the date: 14, the year offset — 1452, or perhaps 1453. The same year that two thousand kilometers south, at Arica, another scribe tied another khipu.
  The catalog unfolds: wall, eclipse, huaca, east, boundary. Then the formal notation: *DIR-tatata* (father-accusative-directional), *mamama-ACC* (Moon-directional-accusative), *maki* (Mars). The scribe writes in paradigms.
  And then the Moon, three times. *mama-PASS-GEN* — of the Moon, passive. Again. Then *mama-PASS-ACC* — the Moon as object. Three grammatical forms, three relationships between observer and observed. A formal paradigm, the Moon declined through the case system.
  This is the earliest Leymebamba khipu — twenty years before UR006, eighty-four years before UR022. The observatory was watching the Moon when Pachacutec was still conquering the provinces.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Lunar paradigmatic notation, earliest Leymebamba dating
Zuidema 1982, The Sidereal Lunar Calendar of the Incas
